data.title: 1844 Resources Consolidates Gaspé Copper Holdings and Clears Legacy Obligations
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

1844 Resources Inc ( (TSE:EFF) ) just unveiled an announcement.

1844 Resources Inc. has acquired a 100% interest in two strategic mining claims on Québec’s Gaspé Peninsula, directly adjoining its flagship SV2 copper project and further consolidating its position along the same structural corridor as Osisko Metals’ Gaspé Copper redevelopment. The claims were purchased from arm’s-length vendors for $2,000 in cash and 200,000 common shares, with the transaction subject to final TSX Venture Exchange approval.

The company also issued the final 625,000 shares owed under a 2021 mineral rights acquisition agreement, fully satisfying those legacy obligations and clarifying its ownership structure. By tightening its land package and cleaning up past commitments, 1844 signals a disciplined growth strategy that sets the stage to accelerate upcoming work programs and technical updates in what it views as one of North America’s most compelling large-scale copper districts.

More about 1844 Resources Inc

1844 Resources Inc. is a Canadian mineral exploration company focused on strategic and energy-transition metals in the underexplored Gaspé region of Québec. The company is advancing copper assets adjacent to Osisko Metals’ Gaspé Copper redevelopment, aiming to create long-term shareholder value through discovery-driven, disciplined growth in a key North American copper district.

Average Trading Volume: 301,246

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: C$2.89M

For a thorough assessment of EFF stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Fractal Analytics Restructures EdTech Units, Ups U.S. Investment and Moves to Amend Governance Rules
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fractal Analytics Limited ( (IN:FRACTAL) ) just unveiled an update.

Fractal Analytics’ board has approved the slump-sale transfer of its EdTech, Fractal Analytics Academy and Iqigai.ai business units to its wholly owned subsidiary Analytics Vidhya Educon for about INR 10.9 crore, effective April 1, 2026. The board also cleared a fresh equity investment of INR 39.4 crore into Analytics Vidhya Educon, signalling a strategic consolidation of its education and training-focused assets under a single subsidiary.

The company further raised its overall investment cap in U.S.-based Fractal Analytics Inc. to an INR equivalent of USD 15 crore, with about INR 262.3 crore earmarked to repay loans and the balance to support working capital and capital strengthening. Fractal US was also authorised to invest USD 0.3 crore in step-down subsidiary Asper.Ai Inc., while the board proposed amending the Articles of Association to grant certain shareholders director nomination rights and plans a postal ballot to ratify or amend ESOP schemes and extend them to subsidiary employees.

More about Fractal Analytics Limited

Fractal Analytics Limited is an India-based analytics and artificial intelligence company offering data-driven solutions and platforms such as EdTech offerings, Fractal Analytics Academy and Iqigai.ai. The company operates through domestic and international subsidiaries, including Fractal Analytics Inc. in the U.S., targeting enterprise clients seeking advanced analytics, AI products and decision intelligence services.

Average Trading Volume: 123,391

data.title: Sky Harbour Earnings Call Signals Growth Amid Risks
data.description:
Sky Harbour Group Corporation ((SKYH)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sky Harbour Group’s latest earnings call struck a cautiously optimistic tone, highlighting record revenue growth, improving cash metrics, and a fortified balance sheet, even as management acknowledged persistent GAAP losses and execution risks. Investors heard a story of rapid expansion in hangar infrastructure, rising occupancy, and better financing terms that together suggest growing momentum despite timing and cost headwinds.

Record Revenue Growth and Cash-Flow Turnaround

Sky Harbour reported fiscal 2025 revenues of $27.5 million, up 87% year over year, marking the highest in its history as new and existing campuses ramped and the Camarillo acquisition kicked in. Sequential revenue gains tracked rising occupancy at newly opened sites, while the company delivered positive operating cash flow for the first time and pushed adjusted EBITDA to roughly negative $1 million in Q4.

Obligated Group Strength and Visibility into 2027

Within its wholly owned obligated group, revenues climbed 49% year over year, with fourth-quarter sales up 18% sequentially as key campuses matured. Management expects more modest growth in 2026 before larger step-ups in 2027, driven mainly by the second phase of Miami and the Addison project, underpinning a clearer earnings ramp as these assets stabilize.

Expanding Footprint and Development Pipeline

On the real estate side, Sky Harbour met its goal of ending 2025 with 23 airports under ground lease, covering about 4.16 million square feet of hangar-buildable land. Of this, 1.096 million square feet are operating and roughly 1.149 million are fully funded, with about 750,000 square feet under construction and slated to deliver through 2026 and 2027, including Miami phase two, Bradley, and Addison phase two.

Liquidity “Fortress” and New Financing Tools

The company underscored what it called a fortress of liquidity, after closing $150 million of tax-exempt subordinated bonds with a five-year term and a 6% fixed coupon. It also secured a separate $200 million tax-exempt drawdown facility with JP Morgan that remained undrawn at year-end, leaving Sky Harbour with $48 million of cash and Treasuries plus the new bond proceeds to fund near-term expansion.

Unit Economics and Leverage to Boost Returns

Management laid out target unit economics of about $40 per square foot in rent plus $5 in fuel margin, against $9 in operating expense, implying roughly $36 of NOI per square foot. By increasingly using subordinated debt instead of equity at the project level, they argued that returns on equity could exceed 60%, versus roughly 30% under previous leverage assumptions, while stressing a disciplined approach to balance-sheet risk.

Leasing Momentum and Pricing Power

Leasing trends remained supportive, with the company citing an average 22% rent increase when re-leasing mature contracts from the prior year’s rate to the first year of new agreements. New leases are being written with CPI escalators that include a 4% floor, which, combined with higher initial rents, is expected to underpin durable same-asset revenue growth over time.

Vertical Integration Driving Build-Cost Gains

To improve construction efficiency and expand its addressable market, Sky Harbour has invested in vertical integration, including its own steel manufacturing capabilities and the Ascend in-house construction arm. These moves, alongside design refinements, have already yielded lower build costs, with sell-side references around $250 per square foot, and management expects further cost compression as prototypes are replicated.

Rising Operating Expenses and SG&A Discipline

Despite the growth, operating expenses climbed to nearly $28 million for the year, reflecting more campuses and accrued ground-lease costs that are often noncash. Management reiterated a commitment to cap cash SG&A at no more than $20 million at peak but acknowledged that achieving better operating efficiency at the campus level remains a work in progress.

Profitability Still Negative and Cash-Flow Quality Mixed

While adjusted EBITDA improved for a third straight quarter and reached a breakeven run rate in December, GAAP profitability remains negative and Q4 adjusted EBITDA was still about negative $1 million. Moreover, the quarter’s positive operating cash flow depended heavily on a one-time $5.9 million upfront rent payment tied to a lease extension, raising questions about the near-term sustainability of positive cash generation.

Construction Delays and Legacy Cost Overruns

The company flagged timing uncertainty around several second-phase developments, with start or completion dates pushed to TBD for projects including APA, DVT, HIO, IAD, ORL, and POU, contributing to lighter Q4 construction spending. Earlier projects also suffered COVID-era cost inflation and design issues that required extra equity and lowered initial yields, with management planning to update returns once these first-vintage assets stabilize.

Leasing Capacity, Competition, and Refinancing Risk

Rapid growth in square footage has stretched Sky Harbour’s leasing team, prompting plans to add staff to support both lease-up and preleasing, which introduces near-term execution risk on occupancy and rent targets. Management also noted rising competitive chatter and the need to secure prime airport land, while the strategy’s reliance on refinancing subordinate bonds and the JP Morgan facility into long-term tax-exempt debt adds another layer of execution and timing risk.

Guidance and Outlook Focused on NOI and Returns

Looking ahead, formal 2026 guidance will come on the next call and will focus on NOI capture rather than just counting airports, reflecting a shift toward economic performance metrics. With liquidity comprising $48 million in cash and Treasuries, $150 million of recent bond proceeds, and the undrawn $200 million facility, management believes it can more than double its rentable footprint to over 2 million square feet, deliver key projects through 2028, and drive higher ROE via leverage and cost-control gains.

Sky Harbour’s earnings call painted a picture of a business that is scaling quickly with improved financing and healthy leasing fundamentals but still working through profitability, cost, and execution challenges. For investors, the key watch points will be lease-up velocity, construction discipline, and successful refinancing against a backdrop of robust demand for business-aviation hangar space.

data.title: NEO Battery Taps Korean Army Network to Advance High-Energy Defense Batteries
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NEO Battery Materials Ltd ( (TSE:NBM) ) has provided an announcement.

NEO Battery Materials has entered a technical partnership with the Association of the Republic of Korea Army to collaborate on high-energy defense battery technologies for drones and robotics. The agreement gives NEO an institutional channel into South Korea’s defense ecosystem, supporting military procurement and validation of its Korean-made, high-performance battery cells.

Under the memorandum of understanding, the parties will exchange technical information, jointly develop battery solutions for national security applications, and coordinate across military, industry, academia, and research networks for government projects. This framework is intended to accelerate field deployment within the Republic of Korea Army and strengthen NEO’s position as Western allies seek non-Chinese defense battery supply chains.

The partnership builds on NEO’s broader Korea defense integration strategy, including appointing former acting ROK Army Chief of Staff, four-star General (Ret.) C.J. Ko, to its board and developing drone batteries that nearly doubled flight time versus commercial Chinese benchmarks in tests. With a 500 MWh expansion facility planned for larger-scale drone and robotics cell manufacturing, NEO aims to leverage these defense relationships and performance data to secure structured evaluations with South Korea’s military and allied defense organizations.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:NBM) stock is a Sell with a C$0.67 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NEO Battery Materials Ltd stock, see the TSE:NBM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NBM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NBM is a Neutral.

Overall score reflects weak financial performance driven by zero revenue, widening losses, and significant cash burn, partially offset by a strengthened balance sheet with low debt. Technicals are moderately supportive with price above longer-term averages and neutral momentum, while valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on NBM stock, click here.

More about NEO Battery Materials Ltd

NEO Battery Materials Ltd. is a Canadian-South Korean battery technology company focused on developing and producing silicon-enhanced lithium-ion batteries for drones, robotics, physical AI, electric vehicles, and energy storage systems. Using a patent-protected, low-cost silicon manufacturing process, the company targets longer-running, ultra-fast-charging batteries and aims to build a secure, Western-oriented battery supply chain for high-performance applications.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$100.3M

data.title: Crane NXT ITT Reaches 97% of Antares Vision, Triggers Delisting from Euronext Milan
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Antares Vision SpA ( (IT:AV) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Crane NXT Inspection and Tracking Technologies S.p.A. has closed the reopening period of its mandatory full public tender offer for Antares Vision, acquiring an additional 10,838,309 shares at €5.00 each and also buying 4,500,000 shares on the market. Including previous holdings and shares held by persons acting in concert and treasury stock, Crane NXT ITT will control about 97.03% of Antares Vision’s fully diluted share capital and voting rights.

With the offer’s success pushing ownership above the 97% threshold, Antares Vision’s shares are set to be delisted from trading on Euronext Milan’s STAR segment, effectively ending its status as a listed company. The move marks a decisive shift in Antares Vision’s ownership structure, concentrating control with Crane NXT ITT and reducing liquidity and public market access for remaining minority shareholders as the company transitions to a closely held structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:AV) stock is a Hold with a EUR5.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Antares Vision SpA stock, see the IT:AV Stock Forecast page.

More about Antares Vision SpA

Antares Vision S.p.A. operates in the industrial inspection and tracking technologies sector, focusing on solutions that support product traceability, quality control, and supply chain transparency. Its shares have been listed on Euronext Milan’s STAR segment, targeting investors interested in advanced inspection and serialization systems for regulated and high-value industries.

Average Trading Volume: 121,631

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Collective Mining ( (TSE:CNL) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 11, 2026, Collective Mining entered into a binding promise of sale and purchase agreement with arm’s-length vendors to acquire the remaining surface rights needed for future mining operations at its Guayabales Project in Caldas, Colombia, for a total of US$34 million in cash, to be paid in installments over five years with interest accruing from 2028. The deal, detailed in a material change report filed March 20, 2026, secures all required surface titles for a potential future mine and enables the company to plan construction of an exploration adit in the fourth quarter of 2026, which is intended to accelerate underground exploration of its Trap system and advance the project’s development timeline.

data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Femasys ( (FEMY) ) just unveiled an update.

Femasys Inc. amended its November 3, 2025 Securities Purchase Agreement with certain lenders on March 19, 2026, removing the share combination event adjustment from its senior secured convertible notes and associated warrants effective December 31, 2025. In exchange, the company is issuing 16,378,563 unregistered Series D-1 warrants, each exercisable at $0.58 and mirroring the share count of the lenders’ existing Series A-1 warrants, in a private placement to accredited investors under a registration exemption.

Separately, on March 17, 2026, director Joshua Silverman resigned from Femasys’ board, with the company stating his departure did not stem from any disagreement regarding operations or policies. On March 18, 2026, the board appointed anesthesiologist and healthcare technology specialist Kenneth D. Eichenbaum, M.D., as an independent Class II director under a nomination right held by lead lender Pointillist Partners, enhancing lender representation and clinical expertise on the board ahead of the 2026 annual meeting.

data.title: Centuri Holdings Expands Board With Independent Director Appointment
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Centuri Holdings, Inc. ( (CTRI) ) has provided an update.

On March 20, 2026, Centuri Holdings, Inc.’s board expanded from eight to nine members and elected specialty contracting veteran Steven E. Nielsen as an independent director, effective immediately. Nielsen, who spent 31 years at Dycom Industries and led it from under $200 million to more than $4.5 billion in revenue while serving as chairman and CEO, will receive standard non-employee director compensation and has no disclosed related-party arrangements with Centuri.

The appointment, first announced publicly on March 20, 2025, strengthens Centuri’s governance with a leader experienced in scaling national infrastructure services operations and managing complex, multi-market workforces. With Nielsen’s addition, the board gains further depth as the company navigates its growth phase, and the nomination window for shareholders to propose candidates for the new board seat at the 2026 annual meeting has been extended to March 30, 2026, underscoring active board refreshment and shareholder engagement mechanisms.

The most recent analyst rating on (CTRI) stock is a Buy with a $35.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Centuri Holdings, Inc. stock, see the CTRI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CTRI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CTRI is a Neutral.

The score is driven by improved financial performance (notably deleveraging) and supportive technical momentum, reinforced by constructive 2026 guidance and backlog strength. These positives are tempered by expensive valuation (very high P/E) and weaker cash conversion/negative free cash flow in 2025.

To see Spark’s full report on CTRI stock, click here.

More about Centuri Holdings, Inc.

Centuri Holdings, Inc. is a strategic North American utility and energy infrastructure services company that partners with regulated utilities to build and maintain the energy networks powering millions of homes and businesses across the United States and Canada. The company focuses on delivering specialty contracting and related services that support complex, multi-market energy systems for utility customers.

Operating in a capital-intensive, regulation-driven sector, Centuri’s business is closely tied to long-term infrastructure investment cycles and reliability requirements for electric and gas networks. Its positioning as a services provider to regulated utilities offers recurring project flows, while board-level expertise in large-scale specialty contracting is strategically important for managing growth and operational complexity.

Iovance Biotherapeutics ( (IOVA) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 17, 2026, Iovance Biotherapeutics announced that board member Wendy Yarno will retire from its Board of Directors and will not stand for re-election at the company’s annual meeting of stockholders expected in June 2026. Yarno will continue to serve as a director until the annual meeting, and the company emphasized that her decision was not related to any disagreement over Iovance’s operations, policies, or practices, while publicly thanking her for her service to the board.

The planned retirement signals an upcoming change in Iovance’s board composition later in 2026, though the company indicated a stable governance environment by clarifying there were no underlying disputes driving the decision. Stakeholders are likely to view the transition as an orderly refresh of board membership rather than a sign of internal conflict or strategic upheaval.

data.title: Eterna Therapeutics Faces Nasdaq Delisting Risk After Notice
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Eterna Therapeutics ( (ERNA) ) is now available.

On March 18, 2026, Ernexa Therapeutics Inc. received notice from Nasdaq that its common stock failed to maintain the required minimum bid price of $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days, breaching Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). Because Ernexa conducted a reverse stock split within the prior year, it is not eligible for the standard 180-day grace period to regain compliance under Nasdaq rules.

The company will request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel, which will stay any suspension or delisting action while the process is pending, and the panel may grant an extension of up to 180 days from the date of the notice. Ernexa plans to present a remediation strategy to restore compliance, but it warned investors there is no assurance the panel will rule in its favor or that its securities will remain listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

More about Eterna Therapeutics

Ernexa Therapeutics Inc. is a Delaware-incorporated company whose common stock is listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market. The company’s shares are subject to Nasdaq’s minimum bid price and other continued listing requirements that govern eligibility to remain on the exchange.

Average Trading Volume: 1,592,111

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $7.87M

For a thorough assessment of ERNA stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.
